title: Build frozen taskset and provider-neutral benchmark harness
goal: Implement the contracts, frozen 24-case taskset, provider-neutral runner boundary, transcript receipts, paired execution plan and offline evaluator required for a real baseline-versus-RepoBrief agent benchmark without claiming a model result.

approval.note: Freeze task definitions, metrics and stop rules before producing any benchmark result. Do not simulate model transcripts or provider token counts.

acceptance.assertion: Versioned machine-readable contracts exist for the fixed taskset, agent-run request, raw transcript receipt, paired result and aggregate evaluation, with explicit negative semantics and size/path limits.

Exactly 24 pre-registered cases cover navigation, structural impact and grounding/freshness across at least three commit-bound repositories, including at least six cases whose correct result requires abstention or a negative claim.
The harness deterministically balances baseline-first and treatment-first order, creates isolated per-condition requests and rejects cross-condition transcript, cache or workspace reuse.

A provider-neutral runner command receives one bounded JSON request and must return provider/model identity, provider-reported tokens, tool-call records, timestamps, exit status and a complete or hash-bound transcript; missing required evidence makes a run invalid.

Baseline permits grep, glob, search and targeted reads only; treatment permits the same tools plus ask_context, RepoBrief resources, grounding_verify and live_freshness. The allowlist is enforced from recorded tool calls.

The deterministic evaluator measures target hits, false hits, citations/ranges, abstention, false confidence, time, tool calls, provider tokens, bytes, stale handling and invalid-run rates, and cannot promote a result when any registered quality or safety regression threshold is crossed.
Synthetic fixtures validate contracts and evaluator behavior but are unambiguously marked synthetic and cannot establish agent usefulness or satisfy the real-run task.
Focused and full Lenskit tests, schema checks, deterministic replay tests, diff review and all repository CI gates pass before merge.

metadata.runner_availability_at_registration: No instrumented provider-neutral agent runner with provider token and immutable transcript evidence was found in the live Lenskit surface.
metadata.outcome: The frozen provider-neutral benchmark harness is implemented and validated. It plans 96 isolated requests across 24 commit-bound cases and two repetitions, rejects manipulated or incomplete run evidence, keeps synthetic fixtures synthetic-only, and preserves default_promoted=false. No real agent usefulness result is claimed.

validation.post_merge_file_readback: merger/lenskit/core/agent_benchmark.py and bound taskset/tests on Lenskit main

verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-contracts: PR #1000 adds versioned Draft-07 contracts for the taskset, run request, run receipt and aggregate evaluation with explicit negative outcomes and bounded data surfaces.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-fixed-cases: The merged taskset contains exactly 24 cases with an 8/8/8 class distribution across Lenskit, Grabowski and Weltgewebe, including abstention, stale, not-comparable and invalid-evidence cases.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-pairing: The planner creates 96 deterministic requests over two repetitions, balances condition order and assigns distinct session and workspace identities; reuse and missing pairs invalidate evidence.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-runner-boundary: The external runner uses an explicit argument array with shell disabled and must provide exact provider/model identity, provider-reported tokens, bounded tool records, aware timestamps and a complete hash-bound transcript.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-tool-policy: Baseline and treatment allowlists are frozen in the taskset and validated against every recorded tool call; RepoBrief tools remain absent from baseline.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-evaluator: The evaluator scores targets, false hits, citations, abstention, false confidence, time, calls, tokens and bytes and blocks utility when a quality or safety regression crosses its registered threshold.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-no-fake-run: Synthetic fixtures are forced to measurement_scope=synthetic_contract_fixture and decision.status=synthetic_only; the merged proof explicitly denies real agent usefulness.
verified_acceptance.rab-v1-t001-tests: All final-head Lenskit CI gates passed, the complete 178576-byte diff was reviewed at SHA-256 69f9eb47..., and merge commit 7b729b52... was byte-read back on main.

next_task.blocker: No qualifying instrumented agent runner with exact model/provider identity, provider token accounting, tool-call capture and immutable transcript binding is currently exposed. Real values must not be simulated.
